'''Andrew Wynyard''' (born 14 February 1973) is a New Zealand [[rugby league]] coach and former player who played professionally in England and Australia.

==Playing career==
He played for the Bundaberg Grizzlies in the 1998 [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>[http://queensland.rleague.com/results/1998/round-19.php "Round 19: July 25, 26"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080517203551/http://queensland.rleague.com/results/1998/round-19.php |date=2008-05-17}} ''queensland.rleague.com''</ref>

Wynyard started his professional career with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] before being signed by the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] midway through the 1999 season.<ref>[http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-5235603.html Broncos go for Wynyard]{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} ''The Evening Standard'', 14 May 1999</ref> That same year, Wynyard was selected to represent [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] against [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]].<ref name="NZ_Herald_50093">{{cite web|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=50093|title=Rugby League: It's a different world for Maori|author=Jessup, Peter|date=3 November 1999|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|accessdate=16 September 2011}}</ref>

Between 1999 and 2000 Wynyard played 40 [[Super League]] matches for the Broncos, before returning to Australia. In 2001 Wynyard joined the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] and went on to play 67 matches for the Dolphins in the [[Queensland Cup]], scoring 14 tries, and being part of champion sides in 2002 and 2003.<ref>[http://www.redcliffedolphins.com.au/index.php?id=345 Andrew Wynyard stats] ''redcliffedolphins.com.au''</ref> Wynyard represented the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] in 2001.<ref>[http://www.redcliffedolphins.com.au/index.php?id=2291 Andrew Wynyard biography] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110222032257/http://www.redcliffedolphins.com.au/index.php?id=2291|date=22 February 2011}} ''redcliffedolphins.com.au''</ref>

Wynyard became the Dolphins Colts assistant coach in 2008 before coaching the [[FOGS Cup]] side in 2009.<ref>[http://redcliffe-and-bayside-herald.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/first-year-success-for-dolphin-coach-andrew-wynyard/ First-year success for Dolphin coach Andrew Wynyard] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110825195419/http://redcliffe-and-bayside-herald.whereilive.com.au/sport/story/first-year-success-for-dolphin-coach-andrew-wynyard/|date=25 August 2011}} ''Redcliffe & Bayside Herald'', 13 July 2009</ref>

Andrew Wynyard is currently the Head Coach for the Brighton Roosters BRL A Grade team which competes in the CAOS BRL Premier A Grade [[Brisbane Rugby League (2001)]] competition as a direct feeder team to the Redcliffe Dolphins Host Plus Cup Statewide Side.{{cn|date=July 2024}}
